sglang.title: [Feat] add llava-onevision, with support for (1) siglip encoder, (2) qwen2 decoder (3) openai api compatible server.
sglang.author: kcz358
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
This is a working PR and I'm putting here to see the changes and welcome any suggestions.
Motivation
With this PR, we wish to add support for LLaVA-OneVision. A new model that accepts multi-type inputs, including single-image with anyres_max_N and multi-image, and video.
Modification

add support fo

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix server crash on empty prompt
vllm.author: maxdebayser
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes #7632
To reproduce, start the server with python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model gpt2 and send an empty prompt:
$ curl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions    -H "Content-Type: application/json"    -d '{
     "model": "gpt2",
     "prompt": [""],
     "max_tokens": 20,
     "temp

vllm.title: [Core] Add multi-step support to LLMEngine
vllm.author: alexm-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds multi-step support to the LLM Engine class. The new multi-step methods that were originally added to async_llm_engine were moved to llm_engine, since async_llm_engine inherits from llm_engine.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix run_batch logger
vllm.author: pooyadavoodi
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The run_batch logger currently does not work (it does not print anything). My guess is the logger created by run_batch conflicts with the logger created by vllm.entrypoints.logger. So this PR removes the new logger created by run_batch and lets run_batch use the logger from Use vllm.entrypoints.logg

vllm.title: [Frontend] Publish  Prometheus metrics in run_batch API
vllm.author: pooyadavoodi
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The run_batch API currently doesn't publish Prometheus metrics because run_batch is not part of api_server that creates a Prometheus client in the server. This PR runs an http server using prometheus_client so that the metrics that get published by LLMEngine at the endpoint /metrics can be served.

vllm.title: [Frontend] add json_schema support from OpenAI protocol
vllm.author: rockwotj
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
OpenAI now supports json_schema and since vLLM already supports this
functionality add the ability to plumb the new OpenAI protocol field to
vLLM's functionality.
FIX #7656
